person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir Peter de Montfort must have been born after 1390/1. His older brother John (on whose death he inherited John's barony) was said to be five at the time of his father's death in 1296.

Sir Peter de Montfort, 3rd Lord Montfort had at least 3 illegitimate children with Lora de Ullenhall, including:

1.) Sir John

2.) Richard

3.) Alice, wife of Fulk de Pembrugge, of Richard de Nowers, of Sir Thomas de Langley, of Sir John Giffard, & of William Thymbleby

His relationship with Lora de Ullenhall must have started before 1313, for in that year he settled an annuity on their oldest child John.

Sir Peter de Montfort, 3rd Lord Montfort married Margaret Furnival, daughter of Sir Thomas de Furnival, 2nd Lord Furnivall and Joane de Verdun, before 1339. 

«b»They had 1 son:«/b»
1.) Guy

"PIERS (DE MONTFORT), LORD MONTFORT, brother and heir. On 14 June 1312, when he must have been under age, he was instituted to the rectory of Ilmington, co. Warwick, being described as "clerk." He was summoned for military service against the Scots 20 August 1316. In 1317, as rector of Ilmington, he had a dispensation for nonresidence from the Bishop of Worcester, and for not taking further orders for three years. On 15 February 1318/9 he was going to Gascony in the King's service in the retinue of William de Montagu, Seneschal of Gascony. He resigned the rectory of Ilmington 5 May 1320, and in October, as Piers de Montfort, knight, he presented his successor. In connection with the Earl of Lancaster's rebellion, Piers was ordered, 30 November 1321, to assemble horse and foot in Warwickshire, &c., to resist the insurgents, and by 10 January 1321/2 was in the Welsh Marches, guarding the border, and four days later was appointed one of the custodians of the city of Worcester. In 1324, as Piers de Montfort of Beaudesert, he settled Remenham and Ilmington on himself and the heirs of his body, with remainder to John, son of Lora of Ullenhall, and his issue, in default to Alice, sister of John, &c. On 20 February 1324/5 he was summoned to a Colloquium with the magnates. In May 1326, as Piers de Montfort, son of Sir John de Montfort, he made a feoffment of various manors. On 18 October 1326 he was given custody of Warwick Castle, the Earl's heir being a minor. He was summoned for service against the Scots again 5 April 1327. On 7 August 1335 he was summoned to a Council at London, and to other Councils in 1342. He was summoned to Parliament from 22 January 1335/6 to 10 March 1348/9. In the autumn of 1338 he went on the expedition to Flanders under William de Montagu, Earl of Salisbury. From 1340 onward he was placed on numerous commissions, of the peace, oyer and terminer, array, &c. He was assessed to contribute 12 men-at-arms in respect of lands in Warwickshire, Notts, Rutland, Surrey, Berks and Wilts, for the campaign of 1346 in France. In October 1350 he had a papal indult to choose a confessor.

He is said to have married Margaret, daughter of Thomas DE FURNIVALLE [LORD FURNIVALLE]. He died s.p.m.s. legitimate, before 24 January 1369/70, when his will (dated 11 December 1367) was proved. At his death any hereditary Barony which may be conceived to have been created by the writ of 1295 fell, according to modern doctrine, into abeyance between his two sisters or their representatives."

He desired to be buried in the Friars Preachers', Warwick. He mentions Dame Lora de Astley, a nun at Pinley, and his kinsman Sir Baldwin Frevyle the elder. To his son Richard de Montfort, and executor, he gave all the silver and gilt vessels, and all goods in the manors of Kyngeshurst and Odes, cos. Warwick and Wilts." 

«b»Death«/b»
Sir Peter de Montfort, 3rd Lord Montfort left a will on 11 December 1367; Requested burial in the Friars Preachers, Warwick. His estate was probated on 24 January 1370.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Piers de Montfort於1292年之後出生在英國瓦維其可爾卡素 利波迪時，他的父親John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert19996歲，母親Alicia Plaunche8733歲。他和Lora de Astley至少有2個兒子。他去世於1370年1月24日之前，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Montfort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Breton: variant of   Monfort   1. This surname is most common in Brittany and Haute-Savoie. See also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort: habitational name from Montfoort in Utrecht. It is a cognate of 1 above (see Monfort 1) and also its altered form (or perhaps vice versa).
English (of Norman origin): very rare variant of   Mountford  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Monfort
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Wilner.

French and Breton: habitational name from any of numerous places called Montfort, from Old French mont ‘hill’ (see Mont 1) + fort ‘strong, impregnable’ (from Latin fortis). This surname is by far most common in Brittany. It is also found in Haiti. Compare Montfort 1, see also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort, a variant of Montfort 2 and also a cognate of the French name in 1 above and an altered form of its variant Montfort 1 (or perhaps vice versa).

variants.name: Monforte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmela, Carmine, Rocco, Rosaria, Sal.  Spanish Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Medardo.

Italian and Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Monforte, from Latin mons ‘mountain’ (genitive montis) + fortis ‘strong’, probably a reference to the strategic position of these locations.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Montfort  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Piers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Regular medieval vernacular form of Peter (from the Old French nominative case, as against the oblique Pier, modern Pierre). In the form Pierce it survived into the 18th century, although in part this may be a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name. Piers was revived in the mid-20th century, perhaps partly under the influence of William Langland's great rambling medieval poem Piers Plowman ( 1367–86 ), in which the character of Piers symbolizes the virtues of hard work, honesty, and fairness.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Piers de Montfort was born after 1292, in Castle Liberty, Warwickshire, England, his father, John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert, was 19996 and his mother, Alicia Plaunche, was 8733. He had at least 2 sons with Lora de Astley. He died before 24 January 1370, and was buried in Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Piers de Montfort est né après 1292, à Castle Liberty, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert, avait 19996 ans et sa mère, Alicia Plaunche, avait 8733 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Lora de Astley. Il est décédé avant 24 janvier 1370, et a été enterré à Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Piers de Montfort nach 1292 in Castle Liberty, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert 19996 und seine Mutter Alicia Plaunche 8733. Er hatte mit Lora de Astley mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb vor 24. Januar 1370 und wurde in Warwickshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Piers de Montfort è nato il dopo il 1292 nel Castle Liberty, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert, aveva 19996 anni e il suo madre, Alicia Plaunche, aveva 8733 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Lora de Astley. Morì prima del 24 gennaio 1370, e fu sepolto a Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Piers de Montfortが1292年以後にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアカストル リバートイで生まれた時，父John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesertは19996歳，母Alicia Plauncheは8733歳であった。彼はLora de Astleyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1370年1月24日以前に死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Piers de Montfort(이)가 1292년 이후 영국 워윅실르 카스틀 리버티에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert(은)는 19996세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia Plaunche(은)는 8733세였다. 그는 Lora de Astley(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1370년 1월 24일 이전 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Piers de Montfort nasceu após 1292, em Castle Liberty, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert, tinha 19996 anos e sua mãe, Alicia Plaunche, tinha 8733 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Lora de Astley. Ele faleceu antes 24 de janeiro de 1370, e foi sepultado em Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Piers de Montfort родился после 1292 г. в местности кастл либирти, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John De Montfort Baron Of Beaudesert было 19996 лет, а матери, которую звали Alicia Plaunche, 8733. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Lora de Astley. Он умер перед 24 январь 1370 г. и был похоронен в местности варвиккшир, Англия.
